rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To First Amended Petition To Obtain Personal Property Under Probate Code Sections 850 And 859
rulings.Rulings: Chicago Title Company's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Petitioner has not stated a claim against Chicago Title under Probate Code sections 850 and 859. Petitioner, a nonparty to the escrow handled by Chicago Title, has pleaded no facts on the basis of which Chicago Title had any duty to Petitioner to investigate the authority of the parties to the escrow or the commercial reasonableness of the transaction in escrow. Nor has Petitioner pleaded any non-conclusory facts on the basis of which Chicago Title could be found to have aided and abetted or conspired with the purported seller. Petitioner has had a prior opportunity to amend to address these issues and has not indicated any other facts he might plead. Given the law governing duties of escrow agents, there is no reason to think another opportunity to amend could cure the defects. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, December 11, 2019.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: Moot in light of ruling on companion demurrer.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer of Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. ("Respondents") is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The allegations in the First Amended Petition are not sufficient to state a claim under Probate Code sections 850 and 859 against Respondents. Petitioner has not pleaded Respondents knew of Mr. Porchia's alleged lack of authority or intent to defraud. Neither obtaining an allegedly below-market price nor turning a quick profit gives rise to a reasonable inference that the buyer conspired with or aided and abetted the seller or imposed on Respondents a duty to Petitioner to investigate. Nor has Petitioner pleaded how Respondents are not entitled to the protections of Probate Code section 18100. Neither agency, conspiracy, nor aiding and abetting may be pleaded with conclusory statements or conclusions of law. If, however, Petitioner can in good faith plead facts that cure the Petition's defects as to Respondents, he may do so within 10 days of this order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3701) no later than 4:00 p.mn. Wednesday, December 11, 2019.
